revision.text:
'''野中宮山古墳'''（のなかみややまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。
==概要== [[古市古墳群]]のほぼ中央、[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の西、[[墓山古墳]]の北に位置する。全長約154ｍ、後円部径約100ｍ、高さ約14.1ｍ、前方部幅約75ｍ、高さ約10.1ｍ。墳丘は三段築成で、くびれ部の両側には造り出しがある。周囲に周濠と堤をめぐらし、堤の外側には区画用と見られる掘り込みが掘削されていることが発掘調査により明らかになっている。現在、後円部上には神社、前方部には幼稚園があるなど、墳丘の改変が著しい。
後円部頂に板石が散乱していることから、竪穴式石槨の存在が推定される。板状の銅製品の出土が伝わっている以外、副葬品は不明。1984、85年の発掘調査により、葺石と円筒[[埴輪]]列の存在が確認された。また、南側の造出しからは、家、蓋、盾、囲み、水鳥、鶏、馬、猪などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの埴輪や、前方部が後円部に対して短小・低平であるという墳形上の特徴から、5世紀前半の築造と推定されている。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
[[Category:古墳|のなかみややまこふん]] [[Category:羽曳野市|のなかみややまこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub|のなかみややまこふん}}

== 立地と規模 == [[古市古墳群]]のほぼ中央、[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の西、[[墓山古墳]]の北に位置する。規模は全長約154メートルであり、後円部は径が約100メートル、高さ約14.1メートル、前方部は幅が約75メートル、高さ約10.1メートルである。
== 概要 == 墳丘は三段築成で、くびれ部の両側には造り出しがある。周囲に周濠と堤をめぐらし、堤の外側には区画用とみられる掘り込みが掘削されていることが[[発掘調査]]により明らかになっている。現在、後円部上には[[神社]]、前方部には[[幼稚園]]があるなど、墳丘の改変が著しい。
後円部頂に板石が散乱していることから、[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]の存在が推定される。板状の銅製品の出土が伝わっている以外、副葬品は不明。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）・[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の発掘調査により、[[葺石]]と[[円筒埴輪]]列の存在が確認された。また、南側の造出しからは、家、蓋、盾、囲み、水鳥、鶏、馬、猪などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの[[埴輪]]や、前方部が後円部に対して短小・低平であるという墳形上の特徴から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と推定されている。

後円部頂に板石が散乱していることから、[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]の存在が推定される。板状の銅製品の出土が伝わっている以外、副葬品は不明。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）・[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の発掘調査により、[[葺石]]と[[円筒埴輪]]列の存在が確認された。また、南側の造出しからは、家、蓋、盾、囲み、水鳥、鶏、馬、猪などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの[[埴輪]]や、前方部が後円部に対して短小・低平であるという墳形上の特徴から、[[5世紀]]前半の築造と推定されている。
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html ライブラリー古市古墳群]（藤井寺市）

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]] '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture. == L..."
revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

revision.comment: テンプレートを加えてみました。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中宮山古墳 |画像=[[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|280px]]
墳丘 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|8.5|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長154メートル |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]か |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末葉 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=銅製品 |史跡=指定なし |有形文化財=指定なし |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=野中宮山
古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=36.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=08.5|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|8.5|E}}}} }} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[はざみ山古墳]]（上）と'''野中宮山古墳'''（下）の空中写真}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''野中宮山古墳'''（のなかみややまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。 == 立地と規模 == [[古市古墳群]]のほぼ中央、[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の西、[[墓山古墳]]の北、[[はざみ山古墳]]の南に位置する。規模は全長約154メートルであり、後円部は径が約100メートル、高さ約14.1メートル、前方部は幅が約75メートル、高さ約10.1メートルである。 == 概要 == 墳丘は三段築成で、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲に周濠と堤をめぐらし、堤の外側には区画用とみられる掘り込みが掘削されていることが[[発掘調査]]により明らかになっている。現在、後円部上には野中神社、前方部には[[幼稚園]]があるなど、墳丘の改変が著しい。 後円部頂に板石が散乱していることから、[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]の存在が推定される。板状の銅製品の出土が伝わっている以外、副葬品は不明。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）・[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の発掘調査により、[[葺石]]と[[円筒埴輪]]列の存在が確認された。また、南側の造出しからは、家、蓋、盾、囲み、水鳥、鶏、馬、猪などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの[[埴輪]]や、前方部が後円部に対して短小・低平であるという墳形上の特徴から、[[4世紀]]末葉の築造と推定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=教育委員会事務局教育部 文化財保護課|url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/sekaiisan/siru/foreignlanguage/languages/japanese/furuichikofungun/Nonakamiyayama.html|title=野中宮山古墳|work=藤井寺市|accessdate=2025-03-10}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html ライブラリー古市古墳群]（藤井寺市） {{DEFAULTSORT:のなかみややまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:古市古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.
== See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century.

[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)
<!--Nonaka Miyama Kofun-->

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|12=draft}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City) <!--Nonaka Miyama Kofun-->

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中宮山古墳 |画像=[[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|280px]]
墳丘 |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|8.5|E|region:JP-27_scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長154メートル |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]か |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末葉 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=銅製品 |史跡=指定なし |有形文化財=指定なし |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=野中宮山
古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=36.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=08.5|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|8.5|E}}}} }} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[はざみ山古墳]]（上）と'''野中宮山古墳'''（下）の空中写真}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''野中宮山古墳'''（のなかみややまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。 == 立地と規模 == [[古市古墳群]]のほぼ中央、[[誉田御廟山古墳]]の西、[[墓山古墳]]の北、[[はざみ山古墳]]の南に位置する。規模は全長約154メートルであり、後円部は径が約100メートル、高さ約14.1メートル、前方部は幅が約75メートル、高さ約10.1メートルである。 == 概要 == 墳丘は三段築成で、くびれ部の両側には[[造り出し]]がある。周囲に周濠と堤をめぐらし、堤の外側には区画用とみられる掘り込みが掘削されていることが[[発掘調査]]により明らかになっている。現在、後円部上には野中神社、前方部には[[幼稚園]]があるなど、墳丘の改変が著しい。 後円部頂に板石が散乱していることから、[[竪穴式石室|竪穴式石槨]]の存在が推定される。板状の銅製品の出土が伝わっている以外、副葬品は不明。[[1984年]]（昭和59年）・[[1985年]]（昭和60年）の発掘調査により、[[葺石]]と[[円筒埴輪]]列の存在が確認された。また、南側の造出しからは、家、蓋、盾、囲み、水鳥、鶏、馬、猪などの形象埴輪が出土した。これらの[[埴輪]]や、前方部が後円部に対して短小・低平であるという墳形上の特徴から、[[4世紀]]末葉の築造と推定されている{{Cite web|和書|author=教育委員会事務局教育部 文化財保護課|url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/sekaiisan/siru/foreignlanguage/languages/japanese/furuichikofungun/Nonakamiyayama.html|title=野中宮山古墳|work=藤井寺市|accessdate=2025-03-10}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html ライブラリー古市古墳群]（藤井寺市） {{DEFAULTSORT:のなかみややまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:古市古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiidera|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] into [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] via bot
revision.text:
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60236111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11645006]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60236111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11645006]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60236111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11645006]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]], west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)

Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中宮山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60236111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}}} [[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hazamiyama Kofun|qq=}} (top) and '''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:NonakaMiyayama kofun park.JPG|thumb|Nonaka Miyayama Kofun]]
'''Nonaka Miyayama Kofun''' (野中宮山古墳) is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpokoenfun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture.
== Location and Size ==
The kofun is situated in the central area of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, west of the {{ill|Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} and north of the {{ill|Hakayama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}. It measures approximately 154 meters in total length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of about 100 meters and a height of approximately 14.1 meters. The front portion is 75 meters wide and 10.1 meters tall.
== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.osaka.jp/9,0,97.html Library: Furuichi Kofun Cluster] (Fujiidera City)
{{Kofun navbar}}

== Overview ==
The mound is constructed in three tiers, with protruding structures (tsukuridashi) on both sides of the constricted area. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankments, and excavations have revealed trenches outside the embankments, likely for demarcation purposes. The mound has undergone significant alterations, with Nonaka Shrine located on the summit of the rear circular mound and a kindergarten occupying the front portion.
Scattered stone slabs on the summit of the rear mound suggest the presence of a {{ill|tateanashiki sekikaku|ja|竪穴式石槨|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateanashiki sekikaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式石室}} (pit-style stone chamber). Few artifacts have been documented, aside from reported finds of copper plates. Excavations conducted in 1984 and 1985 confirmed the presence of fukiishi (paving stones) and a row of cylindrical haniwa. Additionally, figurative haniwa representing houses, lids, shields, enclosures, waterfowl, chickens, horses, and wild boars were discovered in the southern tsukuridashi. Based on these haniwa and the kofun’s form—where the front portion is relatively small and low compared to the rear circular mound—the kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
